title: Weekly Surge In Google Trends Searches For Home Improvement Loan Signals Consumer Stress Lifting Xly
thesis: Rising interest in home improvement loans often signals increased consumer willingness to spend on discretionary housing-related retail items. Increased loan interest suggests consumer confidence in discretionary home-related spending.
signal_logic: If weekly search volume for 'home improvement loan' rises by more than 15% week-over-week
entry_exit: Entry: If weekly search volume for 'home improvement loan' rises by more than 15% week-over-week Exit: Exit after 3 weeks or if search volume declines for 2 consecutive weeks
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Google Trends weekly search interest via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Google Trends weekly data frequently shows >15% spikes in consumer loan interest seasonally and with credit cycles.

schema.idea: Weekly surge in Google Trends searches for 'home improvement loan' signals consumer stress lifting XLY
schema.behavior: Rising interest in home improvement loans often signals increased consumer willingness to spend on discretionary housing-related retail items.

market_impact.rationale: Increased loan interest suggests consumer confidence in discretionary home-related spending.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly search volume for 'home improvement loan' rises by more than 15% week-over-week
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 3 weeks or if search volume declines for 2 consecutive weeks

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Google Trends weekly data frequently shows >15% spikes in consumer loan interest seasonally and with credit cycles.
